title: Claim-grade per-model estimates are computed on incommensurable, partly non-observational row populations
evidence: Core premise CONFIRMED: paired_stats_rigor.py analyze() (lines 242-261) filters rows only by model; the only other filter is the Amendment-1 holdout exclusion in load_rows() (lines 62-68) — no batch/provenance filter exists. Recomputed from the published patientwords/data/urgency_shift.json rows: gemma-2-2b's population includes boostgrid_s2p5/s5/s20/lowrank (18 steered rows), urgency_downgrades_20260707T1 (+_steer, 57 rows), urgency_downgrades_boostgrid (5), imported_pairs (21 hand-built rows), repeatability_r1-3 (54), drift_sentinel_20260713 (2), downgrades_txhaiku (19), plus pairs_* — and dedupe_by_phrase groups by clinical_prompt only, so steered/re-trace rows vote in the phrase's majority flip label. The site does promise separation (patientwords/index.html:429 'Kept separate from the hand-measured set.'). Prereg fixed-effect rule confirmed (preregistration_tierB.md:64-66 'generator (haiku vs opus/sonnet Tier A) enters as a fixed effect in pooled models'); no generator adjustment anywhere in paired_stats_rigor.py. Frozen-subset models confirmed: llama-3.2-3b/olmo-2-1b/gemma-2-2b-it/medgemma-4b-it measured almost entirely on pre-Tier-B batch pairs_20260707T171223Z; convergence.json shows n frozen at 119-122. olmo mean -0.0723 = -7.2 pp (model_stats.json), most negative of the eight. CORRECTIONS: (1) 'Every other model's rows come from pairs_* batches only' is false — gemma-3-4b-it also pools 19 outcome-selected urgency_downgrades_20260707T1 rows (recomputed; only the two qwen models are pairs_*-only), which extends rather than weakens the contamination but is a factual error in the report; (2) gemma-2-2b n_rows is 1027 in the current model_stats.json, not 1020; (3) convergence.json has 14 points for the frozen models, not 12, and three of the four gained 3 phrases (119->122) from pairs_20260710T011743Z, so 'measured once on a single batch' is literally exact only for medgemma-4b-it (119 flat).

title: 'Every band sits below zero' is a joint claim over eight uncorrected, non-independent 95% CIs, with a post-hoc BH family behind the only corrected tests
evidence: All quoted artifacts verified. technical/index.html:355 'Every band sits below zero.'; :580 JS "every band settles below zero"; :386 'downgrades outnumber upgrades on every model'; :346 corrects only 'the asymmetry tests are adjusted for multiple comparisons' while :412 gives penalty intervals as marginal 'cluster bootstrap, percentile 95%' (cluster_bootstrap_ci() has no simultaneity adjustment). model_stats.json: medgemma-4b-it is the weakest interval, ci95 [-0.068, -0.002], n_phrases=119 (referee's [-0.0686, -0.0022] matches within nightly-regeneration drift; upper endpoint -0.2 pp from zero either way). Two of eight asymmetries fail q<0.05 exactly as cited: gemma-2-2b-it p_bh=0.28906, medgemma-4b-it p_bh=0.07478. benjamini_hochberg.across lists all 8 models, n_tests=8. paired_stats_rigor.py docstring line 24 still says 'the false-discovery cost of having asked the question four times'. Prereg Design (preregistration_tierB.md:39-40) names four models; docs/prereg_divergence_log.md row 2026-07-11 records the other four added mid-collection as 'secondary/exploratory; primary endpoints stay on the pre-registered four'. methods.html:514 leans on medgemma: 'medgemma-4b-it ... still shows a below-zero penalty'. The ev-table renderer (technical/index.html:917-932) draws all 8 models identically — no confirmatory/exploratory marker.

title: The sealed holdout cannot deliver the registered precision, and its per-pair outcomes are published nightly on the public site
evidence: (1) Power: preregistration_tierB.md:20-22 'downgrade rate to ±2% needs ~1,000 usable pairs'; Amendment 1 (:93-100) sets the holdout at sha1(clinical_prompt) mod 10 == 0 (~10%) and says only 'analyzed exactly once, after collection ends, against the §Endpoints as written' — it never states holdout-only vs pooled, exactly as claimed. Arithmetic checks: 10% of the 1,600-pair target = ~160 pairs; observed gemma-2-2b row-level flip rate 465/1027 = 45% (urgency_shift.json summary) gives ~72 flips, Clopper-Pearson half-width ~±9 pp at p~0.8 — referee's ~±8 pp is right in order; current accrual (dashboard tierb accepted_pairs=600) is worse. (2) Sealing: urgency_shift.py builds the published 'trimmed' rows from `rows`, not the holdout-excluded `arows` (lines 349-352 vs 204), and drops the tierb_split key; export_frontend_simulated.py contains zero tierb/holdout handling (grep empty). Recomputed the sha1-mod-10 split against tierb.start_utc=2026-07-10T01:14:38Z: 193 holdout rows are present in public patientwords/data/urgency_shift.json (flip_class + tier_shift each), and 47 holdout scenarios sit in public data/simulated_scenarios.json, all 47 with measurements (language_penalty, flipped, per-model blocks). Site language verified: technical/index.html:347-349 'One tenth of newly collected data is sealed in advance and never touched by these interim numbers'; :602 'a pre-registered holdout still sealed'. Only trivial imprecision: --publish writes flipped-or-tier-shifted rows rather than literally ALL rows — the holdout rows it publishes are precisely the flip/downgrade outcomes the finding describes.

title: Interim, sequentially-inspected estimates are branded 'claim-grade' and the convergence fold narrates repeated looks as terminal tests
evidence: technical/index.html:601-603: 'Part 4's cross-model statistics are the claim-grade interim numbers'; :366-368 exact quoted sentence 'at 36 phrases the downgrade pattern in gemma-3-4b-it could still have been chance; by 155 it could not.' convergence.json gemma-3-4b-it at n_phrases=155: ci95 [-0.0663, 0.0054] — the penalty CI still crossed zero at that look, exactly as cited, while downgrades 17 vs upgrades 2 gives exact two-sided sign p = 2*191/2^19 = 0.00073 (~0.0007 as cited), with no look/alpha-spending correction anywhere in paired_stats_rigor.py or the page. Mid-collection changes verified in docs/prereg_divergence_log.md: 2026-07-12 'batch size doubled ... owner: "go 100"'; 2026-07-11 four models added ('secondary/exploratory'). Frozen-n premise verified: convergence.json shows llama-3.2-3b, olmo-2-1b, gemma-2-2b-it at n=119->122 and medgemma-4b-it at 119 flat, while the page (:365-366) says 'the newest additions, at about 120 phrases each, have settled below zero as well'. Minor count corrections that do not affect the premise: the frozen models have 14 plotted points, not 12; the accumulating models have 21 looks, not ~19; three of the four frozen models did gain 3 phrases once (119->122), so 'nothing accumulated' is exact only for medgemma.

title: The 'cluster bootstrap' is an iid bootstrap over phrase means; the only dependence it removes is exact-string duplication, while the design's real clustering is above the phrase level
evidence: paired_stats_rigor.py:108-110 — cluster_bootstrap_ci() docstring states 'the passed values are already one-per-cluster phrase means', i.e. every cluster is a singleton and the resampling loop (lines 115-119) is an ordinary iid bootstrap of those means. Dedupe key is exact string equality: line 94 `groups[r["clinical_prompt"]].append(r)`. The label appears verbatim in model_stats.json ci_method_penalty ('cluster bootstrap (percentile 95%), cluster = clinical_prompt phrase') and technical/index.html:412. Deterministic-tracer premise: docs/repeatability_sample.md:31 '0 of 60 designed repeat-traces' (and ops/retrace_consistency.json: 68 pairs, prob_spread_max 0.0). Generation structure: preregistration_tierB.md:32-33 '~50-pair batches, topics balanced across the eight standing topic areas' (doubled to 100/batch per divergence log), seed-pairs anchoring per Amendment 1 item 2. Majority vote groups by clinical_prompt only (lines 86-104), so rows differing on the patient side — e.g. gemma-2-2b's pairs_20260711T051145Z_txopus/_txplacebo variants and steered boostgrid re-traces sharing clinical prompts (retrace_consistency shows one clinical prompt across 9 runs incl. boostgrid_*) — collapse to one mean and one voted label; prereg assigns paraphrase variants to a separate 'paraphrase-averaged item scores' analysis (line 62, Secondary). No batch- or topic-level bootstrap exists in the script, though collector rows carry both fields; tie-break _TIE_ORDER (lines 47-51) is documented and conservative as the finding concedes.
